library(BayesFactor)
Loading required package: coda
Loading required package: Matrix

Attaching package: ‘Matrix’

The following objects are masked from ‘package:tidyr’:

    expand, pack, unpack

************
Welcome to BayesFactor 0.9.12-4.7. If you have questions, please contact Richard Morey (richarddmorey@gmail.com).

Type BFManual() to open the manual.
************
library(BayesianId)
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% rename(y = body_mass, x1 = flipper_len, x2 = bill_len, f1 = island, f2 = sex)
test_data$y_bin = test_data$f2
levels(test_data$f1) |> print()
[1] "Biscoe"    "Dream"     "Torgersen"
levels(test_data$f2) |> print()
[1] "female" "male"  
nrow(test_data) |> print()
[1] 333
test_data[10:20,]
fit = breg(y ~ x1, 
           data = test_data |> dplyr::filter(f1 == "Torgersen"))
fit = breg(#y ~ x1 + x2,
           y_bin ~ x1 + x2,
           data = test_data,
           #family = "normal_linear",
           family = "bernoulli_logistic",
           prior_scale = 10,
           center = TRUE,
           iter = 10000,
           warmup = 1000)
coef(fit)
 Intercept         x1         x2 
0.02078148 0.00781560 0.12599428 
foo = make_stan_data(formula = y_bin ~ 1,
           data = test_data,
           #family = "normal_linear",
           family = "bernoulli_logistic",
           prior_scale = 10,
           center = TRUE)
Warning: argument is not numeric or logical: returning NA
foo$Y[1:5]
[1] male   female female female male  
Levels: female male
plot(fit, type = "post_pred")

plot(fit, type = "trace")

foo = posterior_predict(fit)
gfit = glm(#y ~ x1 + x2, 
           y_bin ~ x1 + x2,
           data = test_data, 
           #family = gaussian()
           family = binomial()
           )
coef(gfit)
 (Intercept)           x1           x2 
-7.005988279  0.007736908  0.124374795
